pr.title: RHOAIENG-56131: odh-model-controller reconciliation fails when LLMInferenceService is stopped - AuthPolicy targets deleted HTTPRoute
pr.body:
When an LLMInferenceService is stopped via the serving.kserve.io/stop
annotation, the HTTPRoute is deleted as part of the stop cleanup but the
AuthPolicy (owned by the LLMInferenceService) remains. The controller
then fails continuously trying to look up the deleted HTTPRoute that the
AuthPolicy targets, producing noisy error logs and Warning events.

Check the stop annotation at the start of KserveAuthPolicyReconciler.Reconcile
and skip AuthPolicy reconciliation when the service is stopped. The AuthPolicy
is preserved for future use when the service is restarted.

Closes [RHOAIENG-56131](https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHOAIENG-56131)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Signed-off-by: Laurent Tourreau <ltourrea@redhat.com>

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Tests**
  * Added coverage for stopped-service behavior: verifies that when a service is marked stopped and its dependent route is removed, controllers do not produce new reconciliation error events.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Reconciliation now bypasses AuthPolicy updates for services marked as stopped, preventing unnecessary reconciliation and spurious error events during teardown.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
